name: Chile Pepper Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the chiles and peppers with the vinegar in a food processor. Process for 3 minutes until pureed.
2. Pour the puree into a saucepan and add sugar. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, stirring constantly.
3. Remove from heat, skim foam from the top, discard, and stir in pectin.
4. Return to heat and bring back to a rolling boil for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
5. Remove from heat and stir continuously for 5 more minutes while it thickens.
6. Allow to cool before pouring into clean, sterilized jars with 1/4 inch headspace. Seal as desired.
Chile Pepper Jelly is one way of preserving chiles - and it makes a delicious condiment at any meal.
